name: Axelspace GRUS

overview.value: GRUS is a constellation of 80 kg Earth observation micro-satellites developed and operated by Axelspace, equipped with optical telescopes providing 2.5 m ground resolution panchromatic and 5.0 m multispectral imagery with a swath over 50 km, flying in a 600 km sun-synchronous orbit. The first satellite, GRUS 1A, launched on 27 December 2018, followed by four more (1B-1E) launched together on 22 March 2021.

base.reason: established aggregator, single reference (audit Phase 3 backfill, 2026-07-08)

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add. Updated 2026-07-13 (weekly maintenance): Axelspace confirmed on its own site that all seven GRUS-3 satellites reached orbit and returned first signals on the 2026-07-07 Transporter-17 launch (source is first-party, supersedes the Gunter's GRUS-1 page for sats_launched_total and latest_launch_date). sats_launched_total is 5 (GRUS-1, per Gunter's) plus 7 (GRUS-3, per Axelspace's launch-success release) = 12; no single source states a combined total. sats_active_claimed stays at 5 (GRUS-1): the GRUS-3 release states commissioning is underway ahead of imagery service, not yet claimed as active/operational.

events.headline: GRUS 1A launches aboard Soyuz-2-1a from Vostochny, Axelspace's first GRUS satellite
GRUS 1B, 1C, 1D and 1E launch together from Baikonur, completing the five-satellite GRUS-1 constellation
Axelspace closes Series C funding to add five more GRUS satellites to the AxelGlobe constellation
Axelspace starts AxelGlobe Earth observation service with its five-satellite GRUS constellation
GRUS-3alpha launches to validate the optical system for Axelspace's next-generation GRUS-3 satellites
Axelspace confirms July 7, 2026 launch date for seven GRUS-3 Earth observation microsatellites
